questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{5x} - e^{3x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 1} (x^{3} - 1) / (x^{5} - 1).
questions.hint: Use standard limit formula or L'Hopital's rule.
questions.solution: Applying L'Hopital's Rule yields (3x^(2)) / (5x^(4)). At x=1, this is 3/5.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+5)/(x-4))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+3)/(x-6))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+5)/(x-6))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Determine lim_{x -> 0} (sin(13x) - sin(9x)) / x.
questions.hint: Use lim_{x->0} sin(kx)/x = k.
questions.solution: We can split the limit as lim_{x->0} sin(13x)/x - lim_{x->0} sin(9x)/x = 13 - 9 = 4.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 1} (x^{8} - 1) / (x^{5} - 1).
questions.hint: Use standard limit formula or L'Hopital's rule.
questions.solution: Applying L'Hopital's Rule yields (8x^(7)) / (5x^(4)). At x=1, this is 8/5.

questions.question: Determine lim_{x -> 0} (sin(9x) - sin(4x)) / x.
questions.hint: Use lim_{x->0} sin(kx)/x = k.
questions.solution: We can split the limit as lim_{x->0} sin(9x)/x - lim_{x->0} sin(4x)/x = 9 - 4 = 5.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+3)/(x-1))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{7x} - e^{6x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 1} (x^{2} - 1) / (x^{4} - 1).
questions.hint: Use standard limit formula or L'Hopital's rule.
questions.solution: Applying L'Hopital's Rule yields (2x^(1)) / (4x^(3)). At x=1, this is 2/4.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 1} (x^{5} - 1) / (x^{3} - 1).
questions.hint: Use standard limit formula or L'Hopital's rule.
questions.solution: Applying L'Hopital's Rule yields (5x^(4)) / (3x^(2)). At x=1, this is 5/3.

questions.question: Determine lim_{x -> 0} (sin(10x) - sin(1x)) / x.
questions.hint: Use lim_{x->0} sin(kx)/x = k.
questions.solution: We can split the limit as lim_{x->0} sin(10x)/x - lim_{x->0} sin(1x)/x = 10 - 1 = 9.

questions.question: Evaluate lim_{x -> 0} (e^{11x} - e^{6x}) / x.
questions.hint: Use the standard limit lim_{x->0} (e^x - 1)/x = 1.

questions.question: Calculate lim_{x -> infinity} ((x+6)/(x-7))^x.
questions.hint: Use the 1^infinity form, rewrite as e^{lim (f(x)-1)g(x)}.

questions.question: Determine lim_{x -> 0} (sin(5x) - sin(3x)) / x.
questions.hint: Use lim_{x->0} sin(kx)/x = k.
questions.solution: We can split the limit as lim_{x->0} sin(5x)/x - lim_{x->0} sin(3x)/x = 5 - 3 = 2.
